title: cerastium octandrum var. adnivale
intro: Cerastium octandrum var. adnivale, also known as Snow-in-Summer, is a perennial flowering plant that belongs to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae) and is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and is used to treat a variety of health issues such as skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Cerastium octandrum var. adnivale include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine. Cerastium octandrum var. adnivale can be prepared in various herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
